item.title: Aluminum Falls to One-Month Low on Iran Tension, US Rate Outlook
item.description: Aluminum fell to the lowest level in one month after tensions in the Middle East escalated and expectations of US rate hikes damped the demand outlook for industrial metals.

item.title: China Healthcare Stocks Fall to Record Low Valuation on AI Drain
item.description: China’s healthcare stocks have slumped to their cheapest level ever, as a rush into local artificial intelligence beneficiaries draws capital away from defensive sectors.

item.title: India Bank Shares Jump as Deposit, Yield Worries Ease
item.description: Large private banks are trading near their cheapest levels in a decade.

item.title: Hong Kong Will Inspire China’s Confidence on Capital Flows, Says City’s Financial Secretary
item.description: Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan said the city would attempt to “inspire the confidence” of Chinese authorities, after Beijing’s sweeping crackdown on illicit overseas investment cast a chill over banks in the city.

item.title: Prabowo Needs More Than Rate Hikes to Fix Market, Analysts Say
item.description: The surprise hike in Indonesia’s interest rates on Tuesday helped the rupiah rebound from a record low, but analysts say it likely won’t restore long-term investor confidence in President Prabowo Subianto’s management of the economy.

item.title: China’s Zinc Margins Crushed as Fees Plunge to Record Lows
item.description: Chinese zinc smelters are being tested by a worsening feedstock shortage that has driven processing fees to historic lows and wiped out profits.

item.title: A $6 Billion Share Sale Wave in India Signals Deals Perking Up
item.description: A barrage of shares is heading toward investors in India, signaling a pickup in dealmaking at the end of a subdued first half of 2026.

item.title: China’s Consumer Inflation Stalls Even as Factory Prices Surge
item.description: China’s consumer inflation unexpectedly stalled in May even as factory prices gained at the fastest pace in almost four years, as higher commodity costs bring closer the end of a record streak of economy-wide deflation.

item.title: Modular Power Systems Company ERock Raises $600 Million in IPO
item.description: ERock Inc. raised $600 million in a US initial public offering, pricing its shares at the midpoint of its marketed range.

item.title: Japan’s Producer Prices Jump Again on Elevated Energy Costs
item.description: Japan’s corporate goods prices jumped again in May, signaling strengthening inflationary pressures rippling across domestic supply chains as the war in Iran drags on and keeps energy prices elevated.

item.title: Apotex, Backers Raise $932 Million in Canada IPO Priced at Top
item.description: Apotex Health Corp. and some of its backers raised C$1.3 billion ($932 million) in a Toronto initial public offering, the country’s largest since 2021.

item.title: Korea’s Kospi Extends Losses on Chipmakers, War-Tied Jitters
item.description: South Korea’s equity benchmark slipped Wednesday, with chip stocks resuming losses and risk appetite weakening following a US strike on Iran.

item.title: Nintendo Shares Slide After 2026 Game Showcase Disappoints
item.description: Nintendo Co.’s shares fell by the most in a month after the game maker’s presentation of upcoming games came up short on brand-new titles from heavyweight franchises such as Mario.

item.title: Drugmaker Parabilis Raises $745 Million in US IPO, Placement
item.description: Parabilis Medicines Inc., a clinical-stage cancer drug developer, raised nearly $745 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced above its marketed range, and a private placement.

item.title: Singapore Court Moves to Seize Capital A’s Assets in Dispute
item.description: A Singapore court has moved to seize stakes in fintech firm Big Pay and logistics unit Teleport held by Capital A Bhd.’s Move Digital Sdn. to enforce an arbitration award stemming from a shareholder dispute.

item.title: Thailand Pushes EU Free Trade Deal to Reduce US Reliance
item.description: Thailand is racing to secure a free-trade agreement with the European Union as uncertainty over US tariffs pushes countries to forge new economic partnerships, according to a top trade official.

item.title: South Korea Begins Inspection of FX Banks After Won Slide
item.description: South Korea’s central bank and financial watchdog will begin joint examinations of major foreign-exchange banks on Wednesday, the first such inspections in 14 years.

item.title: Alberta to Propose ‘General Corridor’ for Pipeline to West Coast
item.description: Alberta is set to propose a “general corridor” for a planned new million-barrel-a-day oil pipeline to the northern British Columbia coast rather than a specific route, the provincial minister of Indigenous relations said.

item.title: Gold Extends Drop as Renewed US-Iran Clashes Test Fragile Truce
item.description: Gold extended a decline after the US launched fresh strikes against Iran in retaliation for the downing of a military helicopter, jeopardizing efforts to end the war that’s roiled global markets.

item.title: Cuba Poised for Biggest US Fuel Shipment Since Cold War Embargo
item.description: A Florida trading company is in advanced talks to send Cuba the biggest cargo of US fuel since the Eisenhower administration as the island nation contends with an acute energy crisis.

item.title: ASML’s Surge to Record High Masks Lowball Valuation Versus Peers
item.description: ASML Holding NV’s ascent to fresh record highs has been a highlight for European markets, yet it’s done little to improve what is the stock’s cheapest relative valuation in years.

item.title: Malaysia Warns of Fiscal Slippage, Downplays Recent Ringgit Drop
item.description: Malaysia may not meet its fiscal deficit targets for 2026 as the Iran war drives up the cost of fuel subsidies, Second Finance Minister Amir Hamzah Azizan said, adding that longer-term goals remain intact.

item.title: Leonardo’s New CEO Sees Green Light for Space Venture Next Year
item.description: Leonardo SpA’s new chief executive officer expects European regulatory approval for its satellite venture with Airbus SE and Thales SA by the end of next year, clearing the way for one of Europe’s most ambitious defense and space projects.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Fall After Volatile US Session: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were poised for declines after a volatile session on Wall Street, where investors weighed developments in the Iran conflict and looked ahead to data that may show US inflation accelerated in May.

item.title: Oil Climbs After Fresh US Strikes on Iran Over Helicopter Attack
item.description: Oil rebounded after the US military launched strikes against Iran following the downing of an American helicopter, posing a new threat to a fragile ceasefire that’s been tested by recent attacks in the Middle East.

item.title: BOJ Watchers See Two Rate Hikes in 2026, Starting With Next Week
item.description: Bank of Japan watchers are penciling in two hikes to the benchmark interest rate this year — with the first coming next week — as they increasingly see the war in Iran as likely to spark a sustained run of inflation.

item.title: Brazil Rare-Earth Ambitions Clash With Strained Mining Regulator
item.description: Brazil’s aim to become a leader in rare-earths mining is running head-on into budget cuts and staffing shortages at the sector’s regulatory agency.

item.title: Mexico’s Gripe With US Trade Pact: South Korea Has Better Deal
item.description: As the US and Mexico remain locked in trade talks, Mexican automakers have a complaint with the current agreement: They’re paying some higher tariff rates than competitors in South Korea and Japan.

item.title: Super Micro Plans to Raise $7 Billion in Equity for AI Equipment
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc. plans to raise $7 billion through a package of equity offerings to purchase the components needed to fulfill customers’ orders for the company’s artificial intelligence servers.

item.title: China Tightens Its Grip on Billions in Offshore Wealth
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, Beijing is trying to stop money from leaving the country. Why now – and what it means for wealthy investors and global markets.
